title: Detecting signals embedded in visible light
abstract: Multiple luminaires are mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery between each adjacent pair. A different signal is embedded into the visible illumination emitted by each luminaire, which an image processing module can detect from one or more images captured by a camera. An angle-dependent mask is arranged to cover at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery when viewed from the environment through the mask varies as a function of viewing angle, becoming less visible at greater values of viewing angle (being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction and a normal to the plane, line or contour). The image processing module distinguishes between the different signals based on the periphery being visible when captured from less than a predetermined value of the viewing angle.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A system comprising: multiple luminaires (100) arranged to actively emit visible illumination to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery (301) between each adjacent pair (100a, 100b) of the contiguous luminaires, wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; transmission circuitry (104) arranged to embed a different respective signal into the visible illumination emitted by each of the multiple luminaires; detecting equipment (110) comprising a camera (112) for capturing one or more images of a plurality of said multiple luminaires, and an image processing module (114) configured to detect the respective signals from the one or more images of those luminaires; an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) arranged to cover at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery when viewed from said environment through said angle-dependent mask varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour; wherein the image processing module of the detecting equipment is configured to distinguish between the different signals embedded in the illumination from said plurality of luminaires based on the periphery between adjacent luminaires being visible in said one or more images through the angle-dependent mask when captured from less than a predetermined value of the viewing angle. 2. The system of claim, wherein the angle-dependent mask also covers the multiple luminaires. 3. The system of claim 1 or 2, wherein the angle-dependent mask is a diffuser layer (404). 4. The system of claim 1 or 2, wherein the angle-dependent mask is an array of collimating shields (702). 5. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the periphery (301) comprises a visible join or gap (501) between each of the pairs of adjacent luminaires (100a, 100b). 6. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the periphery (301) comprises a strip of light-absorbing material (408) along at least one edge between each of the pairs of adjacent luminaires (100a, 100b). 7. The system of any preceding claim, wherein each of the multiple luminaires (100) comprises a set of light-emitting elements, and further comprises a light-reflecting material (406) between the light-emitting elements. 8. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the detector equipment (110) comprises a mobile user terminal and the camera is a camera of the mobile user terminal. 9. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the luminaires are arranged in said plane. 10. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the luminaires are arranged in said plane or contour, and wherein said plane or contour is a ceiling. 11. Lighting equipment comprising: multiple luminaires (100) arranged to actively emit visible illumination to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour but with a visible periphery (301) between each adjacent pair (100a, 100b) of the contiguous luminaires, wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; transmission circuitry (104) arranged to embed a different respective signal into the visible illumination emitted by each of the multiple luminaires; and an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) arranged to cover at least periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery when viewed from 

 said environment through said angle-dependent mask varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour. 12. Detecting equipment (110) for detecting different respective signals embedded in visible illumination actively emitted by multiple luminaires (100) to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery (301) between each adjacent pair of the contiguous luminaires (100a, 100b), wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; the detecting equipment comprising: a camera (112) for capturing one or more images of a plurality of contiguous ones of said multiple luminaires; and an image processing module (114) configured to detect the respective signals from the one or more images of the plurality of luminaires; wherein the image processing module is configured to perform said detection based on a view, captured in the one or more images, through an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) covering at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour; the processing module being configured to distinguish between the different signals embedded in the illumination from said plurality of luminaires based on the periphery between adjacent luminaires being visible in said one or more images through the angle- dependent mask when captured from less than a predetermined value of the viewing angle. 13. A method of emitting illumination comprising: using multiple luminaires (100) to actively emit visible illumination to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery (301) between each adjacent pair of the contiguous luminaires (100a, 100b), wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; and embedding a different respective signal into the visible illumination emitted by 

 each of the multiple luminaires; where the emission of said illumination comprises emitting the illumination through an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) covering at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery when viewed from said environment through said angle-dependent mask varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour. 14. A method of detecting different respective signals embedded in visible illumination actively emitted by multiple luminaires (100) to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery (301) between each adjacent pair of the contiguous luminaires (100a, 100b), wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; the method comprising: using a camera (112) to capture one or more images of a plurality of contiguous ones of said multiple luminaires; and detecting the respective signals from the one or more images of the plurality of luminaires; wherein said detection is based on a view, captured in the one or more images, through an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) covering at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour; the detection comprising distinguishing between the different signals embedded in the illumination from said plurality of luminaires based on the periphery between adjacent luminaires being visible in said one or more images though the angle- dependent mask when captured from less than a predetermined value of the viewing angle. 15. A computer program product for detecting different respective signals embedded in visible illumination actively emitted by multiple luminaires (100) to illuminate an environment (109), the multiple luminaires being mounted contiguously in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour, but with a visible periphery 

 (301) between each adjacent pair of the contiguous luminaires (100a, 100b), wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; the computer program product comprising code embodied on computer-readable storage and configured so as when run on one or more processing devices to perform operations of: using a camera (112) to capturing one or more images of a plurality of contiguous ones of said multiple luminaires; and detecting the respective signals from the one or more images of the plurality of luminaires; wherein the code is configured to perform said detection based on a view, captured in the one or more images, through an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) covering at least the periphery between adjacent luminaires, such that an appearance of the periphery varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour; the code being configured to distinguish between the different signals embedded in the illumination from said plurality of luminaires based on the periphery between adjacent luminaires being visible in said one or more images through the angle- dependent mask when captured from less than a predetermined value of the viewing angle. 16. A luminaire (100) arranged to actively emit visible illumination to illuminate an environment (109), the luminaire (100) suitable for mounting in combination with other such luminaires (100) as a contiguously-mounted plurality of luminaires (100) in a plane, along a straight line or following a one or two dimensional contour but with a visible periphery (301) between the luminaire and an adjacent one (100a) of the contiguous luminaires when so mounted, wherein visible illumination is not actively emitted from the periphery; the luminaire (100) comprising: transmission circuitry (104) arranged to embed a signal into the visible illumination emitted by the luminaire; and an angle-dependent mask (404, 702) arranged to cover at least the periphery of the luminaire (100), such that an appearance of the periphery when viewed from said environment through said angle-dependent mask varies as a function of viewing angle (500), becoming less visible at greater values of the viewing angle, the viewing angle being the 

 angle between a corresponding viewing direction (504) and a normal (502) to said plane, line or contour at a point at which the viewing direction intersects the plane, line or contour.
